name: Zep
tagline: Context engineering platform that gives AI agents long-term, relationship-aware memory
seoTitle: Zep - Long-Term Memory for AI Agents
metaDescription: Zep is a context engineering platform that builds a temporal knowledge graph from chat, business data, and events to give AI agents accurate long-term memory.
overview: Zep is a context engineering platform that gives AI agents long-term memory. As your app sends it chat messages, business data, documents, and events, Zep extracts the relationships between them and keeps a temporal knowledge graph that tracks how that context changes over time. When the agent needs to respond, Zep returns pre-formatted context blocks built for the LLM.

It is aimed at developers building production AI assistants and agents that need to remember facts about users and how those facts evolve, rather than re-reading the whole conversation on every turn. You add context as events happen and retrieve assembled, relationship-aware context when you call your model.

In the agent-memory category, Zep sits as a memory framework backed by Graphiti, an open-source temporal knowledge graph engine. Each fact carries valid_at and invalid_at dates, so the graph can express when something became true and when it stopped being true. Zep is delivered as a managed cloud service with SDKs for Python, TypeScript, and Go.
features: Temporal knowledge graph (powered by Graphiti) that tracks facts and relationships with valid_at / invalid_at dates as they change over time
Graph RAG retrieval that returns relationship-aware context instead of raw chat logs
Ingests multiple sources: chat history, business data, documents, and app events
Pre-formatted context blocks built for direct use in your LLM prompt
SDKs for Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, and Go
Managed Zep Cloud service with sub-200ms retrieval latency and SOC2 Type 2 / HIPAA compliance

gettingStarted.intro: Zep is delivered as a managed cloud service. Sign up for Zep Cloud, then install the SDK for your language and feed context to your agent.

steps.heading: Sign up for Zep Cloud
steps.body: Create an account at getzep.com to get an API key. Zep is a managed service, so there is no server to run yourself.

steps.heading: Install an SDK
steps.body: Install the client for your language. Python, TypeScript/JavaScript, and Go are supported.
steps.code:
# Python
pip install zep-cloud

# TypeScript / JavaScript
npm install @getzep/zep-cloud

# Go
go get github.com/getzep/zep-go/v2

steps.heading: Add and retrieve context
steps.body: Feed chat messages, business data, and events to Zep as they happen, then retrieve assembled, relationship-aware context when you call your LLM. See the SDK docs at help.getzep.com for the exact client calls.

useCases: Give a chat assistant long-term memory of user facts and preferences across many sessions
Track how facts change over time (e.g. a user's plan, address, or goals) using the temporal knowledge graph
Assemble relationship-aware context from chat plus business data and documents before each LLM call
Add memory to agents built on frameworks like LangChain, LlamaIndex, or AutoGen via the example integrations
